title: The wreck, third time
why.empty: The editor is empty. Deleting is especially pointless here — the code WORKS. There is no broken tag to remove. Bring it back.
why.not-closed: Something is open and never closed.
why.mute-image: Something on the page is mute. If it fails to load — there will be nothing where it stood. And for half the people, it is nothing always.
hint2.mute-image: There is one element that shows something without describing it.
hint3.mute-image: Close your eyes. Which element on this page simply vanishes for you?
hint4.mute-image: The image has no alt. Describe what is on it — not "photo", but what exactly.
why.local-path: One path points at a computer that is not theirs.
hint2.local-path: Look at the addresses in the menu. One of them looks different from the other.
hint3.local-path: If you sent the folder to a friend, which link would break for them?
hint4.local-path: contact.html sits next to index.html. So the path to it is written that way — just the name.
why.click-here: There is a link whose words do not say where it leads. Read them alone, outside the sentence.
hint2.click-here: A blind person pulls all the links into a list and reads it. How does your list sound?
hint3.click-here: The sentence already says where the link goes. You just did not wrap the tag around the right words.
hint4.click-here: "More about this model" — there are the words. Make the link out of those.
why.no-nav: There is a group of links claiming to be a paragraph.
hint2.no-nav: There is something at the top that LOOKS like a menu.
hint3.no-nav: Three things are missing there: the navigation tag, the list, and the removal of something you typed by hand.
hint4.no-nav: The paragraph becomes a <nav>, with a <ul> inside, each link in its own <li>. The bars go — they are CSS.
why.mute-iframe: There is a window into someone else's page that does not say what is inside.
hint2.mute-iframe: A blind person reaches it and hears… nothing. They do not know whether to enter.
hint3.mute-iframe: The attribute is called title. Careful: not the one in the <head> — the names just happen to match.
hint4.mute-iframe: Add a title to the <iframe>. Describe what it shows.
why.lost-content: You deleted something. And there is nothing to delete here — the code WORKS. Nothing in it is broken. What is broken is WHO it works for. Bring back what you removed and fix the way it is said.
hint2.lost-content: Something is missing from: the image, the embedded window, the three links, the heading. Compare with the original code.
blocks.0.text: A third wreck. This time the code WORKS. Open the preview — the page shows, nothing crashes, everything looks fine.
blocks.1.text: And yet it is broken in several places.
blocks.3.text: Here is the difference from the first two wrecks. There the code was wrong and it showed.
blocks.4.text: Here everything is visible — as long as you look with your eyes, sit at your own computer, and have a fast connection.
blocks.5.text: Take away any one of those three and the page falls apart.
blocks.7.text: Go through the code and ask of each thing: does this work for someone who cannot see the screen? Does it work on another computer? Does it work if something fails to load?
blocks.8.text: The tests are hidden. They will give you a direction, not an answer.
blocks.9.text: Code that works only on your machine is not working code. It just has not reached anyone yet.
blocks.10.text: And this time deleting is even more pointless than before. There is no broken tag. Every line works. Remove something and you are not fixing anything — you are just throwing away part of the page.
